hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext: STANDING WORKFLOW DEFAULT (user preference, every session): Route substantive development work through /do-me (or the obvious domain skill directly); batches through /loop-me; keep trivial single-step changes inline with no ceremony. The -me family runs FIRE-AND-FORGET by default — after you state the task, it works to a finished, committed result and gives you ONE review packet at the end, with no mid-run approval prompts. Safety is enforced mechanically during the run (mechanical GREEN commit gate, ASK-tier permissions for destructive/DB/publish/push ops, staged-secret guard, protected-path parking, blocked-on-fact typing, run-ID revert) and reviewed once after. The full policy is DISPATCH.md §0 (the Autonomy Contract) at ~/.claude/skills/do-me/references/DISPATCH.md — the registry wins over any skill's own gate text. Add 'manual' to any -me invocation (e.g. /do-me manual) to restore per-gate checkpoints for that one run. This is a default, not a mandate — override it for any given task.
